reforms.description: Slovakia aims to increase R&D spending, which is currently low compared to other OECD countries, by redesigning financial support for private R&D and clarifying eligibility rules for innovation support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending is expected to enhance innovation capacity and improve economic performance by fostering private sector investment in research.
reforms.status_evidence: The need for a coherent national innovation plan and redesign of R&D support was highlighted.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical gap in R&D investment, which is essential for enhancing Slovakia's innovation ecosystem and overall economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: R&D activities are relatively underdeveloped in Slovakia with total gross expenditures on R&D the second lowest in the OECD.

reforms.description: Slovakia plans to develop a knowledge society by improving tertiary education quality and facilitating knowledge transfers between academia and the business sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing education and knowledge transfer is expected to build a more skilled workforce, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: To raise its innovative and adaptive capacities, Slovakia needs to develop a knowledge society.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is crucial for addressing the low levels of tertiary educational attainment and weak ties between academia and industry, which are vital for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Slovakia needs to develop a knowledge society by increasing the quality of tertiary education, removing barriers to knowledge transfers and obstacles to human capital accumulation.

reforms.description: Slovakia plans to support innovation and R&D spending as part of its transition to a greener economy, which includes strengthening the protection of property rights and developing the venture capital market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D spending is expected to enhance innovation capacity, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to support innovation and R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards prioritizing innovation and R&D in the context of sustainable economic growth, which is crucial for long-term competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: This requires addressing environmental externalities by extending environmental taxation and removing subsidies, together with improving the adaptive capacities of the economy through eco-innovation.

reforms.description: The Slovak government plans to foster innovation by promoting cooperation between research institutions and enterprises, while also monitoring the effectiveness of existing public funds spent on R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between research and industry is expected to lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to foster innovation in its reform agenda.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to enhance the link between research and industry, which is crucial for driving innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Fostering innovation by promoting co-operation between research institutions and enterprises and monitoring the effectiveness of the existing public funds spent on R&D.

reforms.description: Start-up subsidies were introduced in Slovakia in 2004 to encourage self-employment among job seekers, with conditions relaxed during the crisis to allow school graduates to apply immediately upon registering at the labor office.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The subsidies enhance the financial viability of starting new businesses, thereby promoting entrepreneurship and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The number of supported new self-employed persons reached more than 12,000 in 2009.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly increased the number of self-employed individuals, contributing to the labor market's flexibility and resilience during economic downturns.
reforms.source_quote: Start-up subsidies provided by the labour office increase the financial attractiveness of self-employment, at least in the first years.

reforms.description: The Slovak government introduced permanent measures to stimulate research and development carried out by businesses, which include state budget subsidies and income tax relief.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are designed to enhance private sector R&D investment, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures were part of the government's fiscal stimulus packages and were executed during the crisis.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of subsidies and tax relief for R&D represents a significant policy shift aimed at enhancing innovation capacity in the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: Stimuli for research and development carried out by businesses, provided in the form of state budget subsidies and income tax relief.

reforms.description: The Slovak authorities intend to increase the efficiency of the revenue system by moving to integrated revenue collection under the UNITAS project, which aims to merge tax and customs administrations and establish a new system for managing government revenues.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the efficiency of tax collection, the reform is expected to enhance public sector productivity and free up resources for other growth-enhancing initiatives.
reforms.status_evidence: The first stage of the UNITAS project is to be implemented by 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: The UNITAS project represents a significant overhaul of the revenue collection system, aiming for substantial efficiency gains and improved public sector performance.
reforms.source_quote: The first stage, UNITASI, is to merge the existing tax and customs administrations and to establish a new system for the management and organisation of the collection of government revenues.

reforms.description: Slovakia is implementing reforms to support innovation and R&D spending, focusing on making existing public support more efficient and strengthening property rights.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D funding efficiency and property rights protection is expected to stimulate innovation, leading to economic growth and the development of new technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The government planned reforms to support innovation and R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards enhancing the efficiency of public R&D support, which is crucial for fostering innovation in a transitioning economy.
reforms.source_quote: Reforms to support innovation and R&D spending, such as making existing R&D public support more efficient, strengthening the protection of property rights and developing the venture capital market are needed.

reforms.description: Slovakia introduced feed-in tariffs for electricity production from renewable energy sources (RES) to encourage investment in clean energy technologies. These tariffs guarantee a fixed price for electricity produced from RES for 15 years, promoting the development of biomass and solar energy production.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The feed-in tariffs provide financial incentives for the adoption of renewable energy technologies, which can lead to increased investment and innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The feed-in tariffs were introduced in January 2006 and have been in effect since then.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of feed-in tariffs represents a significant policy shift towards supporting renewable energy, which is crucial for meeting EU commitments and fostering innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.source_quote: Feed-in tariffs for electricity production from RES and from high-efficiency cogeneration were introduced in the electricity sector in January 2006.

reforms.description: The Slovak government has increased the use of subsidies to foster the development of renewable energy sources, including biomass and solar energy production, as part of its commitment to EU directives.
reforms.component_name: Subsidies for Biomass and Solar Energy
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Subsidies for renewable energy projects stimulate investment and innovation in clean energy technologies, contributing to sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Slovak government has been allocating subsidies to develop biomass and solar energy production since 2009.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in subsidies for renewable energy sources is a significant step towards enhancing the country's energy sustainability and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: Slovakia has been allocating subsidies to develop biomass and solar energy production since 2009.

reforms.description: Slovakia needs to implement a framework for eco-innovation to integrate green technologies into the national framework and adapt to new environmental constraints, which would improve the overall functioning of the economy and its adaptability to structural change.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Fostering eco-innovation is expected to create new sources of growth and improve the adaptability of the economy to structural changes, thus enhancing productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The need for a framework for eco-innovation was emphasized as crucial for addressing economic and environmental challenges.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change aimed at enhancing Slovakia's innovation capacity and addressing environmental challenges, which is critical for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Fostering eco-innovation requires implementing structural reforms to foster general innovation capacity rather than targeting R&D efforts on specific fields.

reforms.description: Slovakia's innovation policy was formalized in the 'Strategy of Innovation Policy of the Slovak Republic for 2008-10', adopted in 2007, which aimed to enhance the country's innovative performance through various measures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to improve the innovation ecosystem, which is essential for enhancing productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was adopted and aimed at improving innovation performance.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant shift in prioritizing innovation at the national level, aiming for systemic improvements in the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Pro-innovative measures appeared after 2000, as innovation became a top priority in 'the Competitiveness Strategy for the Slovak Republic until 2010' and in the 'Strategy of Innovation Policy of the Slovak Republic for 2008-10' adopted in 2007.

reforms.description: The Slovak government introduced tax incentives for R&D activities starting January 1, 2010, to complement existing direct public funding for business R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Tax incentives are expected to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, thereby enhancing innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Tax incentives have been introduced to support R&D activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of tax incentives represents a significant enhancement of the existing funding framework for R&D, aimed at increasing private sector engagement.
reforms.source_quote: Since 1 January 2010, tax incentives have also been introduced in complement to direct public funding of business R&D via subsidies.

reforms.description: Slovakia participates in the JEREMIE initiative, aimed at improving access to finance for SMEs in less developed regions of the EU by providing guarantees for loans and venture capital finance.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the volume of credit available to SMEs, the initiative supports innovation and entrepreneurship, which can lead to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The project consists of providing guarantees for loans and venture capital finance to SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance financial support for SMEs, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Slovakia participates in the Joint European Resources for Micro to Medium Enterprises (JEREMIE) initiative, a European programme to improve access to finance for SMEs in the less developed regions of the EU.

reforms.description: The Slovak government plans to support highly innovative firms by providing 'risk capital' as returnable financial aid, aimed at developing the venture capital market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing risk capital to innovative firms can stimulate investment in new technologies and business models, enhancing overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to support highly innovative firms by providing 'risk capital' as returnable financial aid.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is a significant step towards enhancing the financial landscape for innovative firms, which is essential for fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to support highly innovative firms by providing 'risk capital' as returnable financial aid.

reforms.description: Slovakia is establishing a National System of Occupations to better assess and anticipate labor market needs, which includes a focus on 'green skills' relevant to new technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning education and training with market needs, the initiative enhances the skill set of the workforce, which is crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Slovakia is establishing a National System of Occupations, which provides a description of occupations and the related qualification requirements.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for improving the alignment of education with labor market needs, which is essential for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Slovakia is establishing a National System of Occupations, which provides a description of occupations and the related qualification requirements in co-operation with the private sector.

reforms.description: Slovakia intends to better target public funding on isolated areas not served by private operators to promote greater broadband deployment and develop access to broadband infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband access enhances communication efficiency and supports innovation across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: Slovakia intends to better target public funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses a significant infrastructure gap that can facilitate broader access to technology and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Slovakia intends to better target the public funding on isolated areas not served by private operators.

reforms.description: Slovakia aims to ensure a long-term support to innovation and R&D by increasing the weight of R&D in public expenditures and better coordinating innovation policy across government bodies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding can enhance research capabilities and stimulate innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: Ensure a long term support to innovation and R&D by effectively increasing the weight of R&D in public expenditures.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D funding, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Ensure a long term support to innovation and R&D by effectively increasing the weight of R&D in public expenditures.
